title: Level 403
wikitext:
{{Liminal Theme}}{{DISPLAYTITLE:> ERROR 403 FORBIDDEN}} {{Background|file=Level403.jpg}}
The light lies over those halls.
Feeling lingers within the standing walls.
The air is filled with the same emptiness
where every sound dissolves into quietness.
Corridors carry their paths into sorrow,
with footsteps endlessly following tomorrow.
Stars pass one by one at every turn.
Alas, their spirits of light still burn.
In the distance, the lights remain forbidden.
Rest fades away where silence has hidden.
There is a sense of unbelonging here.
Perhaps — what is it you truly see there?




How do you tell someone something about somewhere when the picture consists of thousands of someone's mind? One experiences and finds themselves in that dream-like landscape. You can see they subjectively/objectively took everything their eyes viewed in the place into a sandbox imagery — somehow reminiscent of their mindscape. No need to step into the actual threshold; as of writing this, you can already feel immersed in experimental, whereas the sensation of matter confounded your mind. What exactly makes those rooms and hallways so odd, even when no messages can fully acknowledge the whole bizarre encounter? Feelings and emotions that one cannot pinpoint. People still struggle to reach this ever-so-called message, so how come one individual can compromise it entirely? Thousands of voices that cannot understand the straightforward facade of the spaces and rooms.




If you feel like things about this place are so complicated or hard to understand, maybe it is best not to step upon them.





[[File:Question_mark.png|75px|link=]]
[[File:WhiteQuestion_mark.png|75px|link=]]

'''>LOAD_BACKUP_FILE::SYS://SYSTEM32/ARCHIVE/LEVEL_403_RESTORED.LOG'''

Undetermined










{| class="article-table" !NOTICE: If you enter a gateway opening to another level that doesn't lead you there, but somewhere else, you may have encountered this level. Under no circumstances should entry be attempted, as no confirmed exits currently exist and exploration yields no new areas, resources, or points of interest; the level functions entirely as a dead end and is regarded solely as a forbidden location and warning to wanderers. If an exit in your vicinity redirects here, avoid entering and seek an alternative exit, or wait several days until the gateway returns to its original destination. It is also noteworthy that interactions with this level appear to occur exclusively on an individual basis, though the reason for this phenomenon remains unknown. !
[[File:Question_mark.png|left|150px|link=]]
[[File:WhiteQuestion_mark.png|left|150px|link=]]
|} == Forbidden In The Sense == One question remains among wanderers, and those who stepped into this unknown threshold: is this place forbidden to human presence? {{User:WindXD/Template:BoxImage|file=Level403.jpg}} Chances are, the doors one opens will lead one to destinations that aren't supposed to be there in the first place. If one is unfortunate enough to come across this place, the first thing that unfolds is an agglomeration of empty rooms and hallways, arranged together into an interior landscape of walls and floors. Each area resembles typical commercial buildings connected to one another, where the place is used to remember something in the past. The more time it remembers something, the less it does. Although, despite the overwhelming emptiness, there never seems to be monotony in this repetition. It is hard to tell what people exactly see here. Rooms and hallways manifold into a labyrinth that extends in all directions. The architecture created itself through abnormal spatial logic that no human can ever construct. The kinds of aspects that present here and there, which everyone would encounter during their time inside the indoor maze of this dimension. Thus, unfortunately, most entrants are unable to notice that they have crossed into an entirely different region. Stepping into this location feels like an imperceptible transition; one gradually realizes that their surroundings will begin to change, despite still retaining the same general form, ranging from the ambiance becoming somewhat distant, the air denser and strangely motionless, to the skeletal structure feeling weirdly unfamiliar. Others may believe these areas are not true locations, but are a transition itself between somewhere or sort of something, calling it simply liminal space. A consequence that you know only comes from the act of its passage. The layout of this place holds an indefinable abnormality that most passersby struggle to articulate. As nothing appears overtly impossible, the widespread void manifesting the environment causes every surface oddly conspicuous, where one can point out every feature in every little inch of the vicinity into the list. Deadened, desaturated greenish-tan wall extends throughout the structure, bleaching away its color into the corners. The polished concrete flooring with a reflective sheen akin to stagnant water or darkened glass, occasionally featuring floor tiles atop. A series of circular LED fixtures on the ceiling casts a faint glow into rooms and spaces, attempting to replace the surrounding darkness. Doors sometimes emerge intermittently along the walls, giving the feeling of an undiscovered area beyond even unopened ones. {{User:WindXD/Template:BoxImage|file=Level403-3.jpg}} Interconnected rooms and corridors are arranged together without intelligible planning, forming areas that vary greatly in scale and structure. Instances include narrow passages with very low ceilings abruptly open into massive venue-like halls, corridors stretch to the point that their end becomes hardly visible to anyone, and certain hallways widen to such a degree that their appearance inclines to incomplete rooms by definition. Pillars, arches, recessed cavities, and ornamental curvatures appear throughout the region, serving no purpose, as if the architecture tries to mimic humans' handiwork. Additionally, the layout frequency bends back and distorts through recursive turns, circular pathways, dead-end intersections, and disproportionate structural features. Traversal should be a familiar experience of navigating an endless interior dimension comprised of vacant rooms and corridors. Yet after only a brief period in here, a strong dissonance of something within the structure has deviated from how space should naturally behave. What could go wrong here?









== Familarity In The Concrete == Something is telling that this place is not merely another abandoned interior. The more times it delivers a sense that the place itself is somehow forbidden — not dangerous in any way. In another sense: why here? {{User:WindXD/Template:BoxImage|file=Level403-1.jpg}} Many feel they do not belong here; in other words, the place appears forbidden in some way. It should be ordinary indoor buildings similar to those on other levels, yet everything here appears disconcerting. People describe an inability to isolate what exactly feels wrong. They can point out every visible detail of the environment while unable to explain the strange sensation behind it — some call it an emotional quality. Strangely, many details suggest there is tranquility behind this forbiddance. Walking here can feel like strolling through empty hotel hallways at midnight. You are aware that you shouldn't be walking during this time, yet you can't help but; there is a calm, night feeling from it. Why does it look so depressing yet peaceful in the meantime? The dim greenish-gray/clay-tone atmosphere of the place creates a dark aesthetic akin to after-hours places. Oddly, one can feel comfort even being in a place devoid of life. The same can be said with the aforementioned case; you get that self-awareness of sorrow that those good moments are ending soon during the vacation, yet this emotion never entirely descends into sadness, as there is a calm acceptance between loneliness and comfort. What the meaning of this place might be remains unknown. Whether the place is truly forbidden or presented as error in design, no one really understands it. Frigid air covers the interior produced by countless air-conditioning units installed throughout the ceilings, slowly flowing, filling spaces and rooms; its circulation becomes the only thing animate amidst the stillness of the surrounding concrete. Ventilation shafts disperse the frigid draft squarely across the place, seeping into the alcove and corner. Some areas feel comfortable cold, some areas feel a little cold — it seems reminiscent of over-conditioned hotels during late hours of the night. Nearby, indoor features, including fire sprinklers, smoke detectors, speakers, wireless routers, EXIT signs, fire extinguishers, fire alarms, and emergency cabinets, remain affixed to the walls and ceilings. It may hint that the building had once served a function before being silently vacated, whereby nothing now appears decayed beyond age itself. It feels like any intrusion now feels inappropriate, hence the sense of forbiddance that the place prefers to be undisturbed. {{User:WindXD/Template:BoxImage|file=Level403-5.jpg}} There are so many sensations one can notice here, aside from those rooms, concrete walls, polished floors, and glowing lights. Yet the atmosphere have that profundity without explanation — the never land. The cold air seeps into the place's texture until every surface radiates a faint frigid softness upon contact. Ceiling lights shimmer on the floor, creating a liquid-like surface of luster. When corridors twist and turn in other directions, each row of lights emerges one by one around the curves as if isolated streetlamps pass one after another through the windshield while driving on deserted highways at night. In another sense, some described the imagery before their eyes as an expansive ocean illuminated by stars above. Nobody knows why such imagery arises from the place so ostensibly vacant; one often recounts the experience of being here as bizarrely oneiric. The atmosphere evokes emotions that no language can pinpoint, and walking here can be described as wandering through layers of a dream, forming from solitude itself.









{{User:WindXD/Template:BoxImage|file=Level403-2.jpg}} What truly defines its state as forbidden when there is nobody or anything to stop one, aside from the emptiness that constantly appears every turn they make. Nearly all documented individuals describe having this experience shortly after entering, despite being unable to explain its origin. It is like the instinctive thought buried deep in conscious thought — an acknowledgement of something before concrete evidence even materializes. In time, some acclimate to the atmosphere and continue onward in awareness, while others attempt fixatedly to understand the source of their unease with this place itself. What exactly do you see again? The structural pillars that hold endless tons of weight? Fire alarms and abandoned emergency fixtures that are too sluggish to respond to anything? Soft light that continues to rebound endlessly across polished textile? Mirror-like plane that is too frail to bear the weight of truth or reason? So-and-so? Commonly referred to as noninterference, it has remained everlastingly undisturbed and now wishes to preserve that stillness, and being in this place feels like being in a dreamlike mindscape. It suddenly feels like you are the first one to ever find this place. It makes you feel special. Whatever this feeling originates from, one's mind or the level itself, numerous accounts always have the same interpretation: this place possesses an inherent distaste toward disturbance, and by extension, toward people themselves. Hence, it is forbidden.









== Forgotten In The Abyss == {{User:WindXD/Template:BoxImage|file=Level403-7.jpg}} Individuals should exercise caution during expeditions. After traversing long distances, the area gradually loses what little liveliness it once had. The polished floors dull into opaque concrete, the walls begin to deteriorate, and the electric appliances on the ceiling become out of order. Reflective textures disappear progressively until nothing else travel throughout the corridors, excluding the person itself. Eventually, entire sections succumb to blackout conditions. At first, few to one ceiling lights may remain visible in the surroundings, but continued travel causes even these final lights to vanish one by one until nothing remains aside from never-ending darkness that will surround — remember that all stars that go too far will eventually die. Disturbingly, reports from far-reaching expeditions indicate that the place becomes more architecturally recognizable as it deteriorates further. Carpeting, wallpaper, and frameworks from offices, hotels, or other buildings appear throughout the labyrinth. Therefore, certain regions may vaguely resemble these counterpart complexes before a sudden shift into other institutional interiors; otherwise, they cannot be properly classified due to heavily disordered arrangements of indoor areas that are even worse than before. Areas may unexpectedly shift in elevation, lead to dead ends, or become too narrow, requiring individuals to sidestep, crawl across, or climb with proper equipment. Furthermore, these structures can become hazardous, as the unstable geometry impedes navigation and exposes individuals to accidental falls within this poorly visible terrain. It doesn't matter how much the place has changed because one thing remains clear: no matter how far one goes from the entrance, the place will always appear empty in any way they try to be. Within this abyss-like area, the entire outer sectors are eventually covered in complete darkness as accounts from those who have traversed these areas frequently describe a tangible presence of something unknown. Others describe the sheer magnitude of the surrounding blackness as a chilling magnetism-like force. Despite the place give off the emptiness but noone feel like they are truly alone here; footstep emit more echos than one's own movement should make, faint sound sporadically heard from the distance in the void of metallic reverberations or brief movement beyond, and staring in the abyss of pitch-dark beyong feel like being stare in return from the darkness within with these feeling of not being alone and being watch presence in many ways and times throughout the period residing in this faraway location from the entrace. They mention this fear every now and then, and they are widely present in many more instances throughout the time in this faraway location. {{User:WindXD/Template:BoxImage|file=Level403-6.jpg}} Perhaps it's not something here that doesn't want one here, but the whole place. More often, the increasingly distorted layouts and oppressive structural irregularities found within the distant sectors produce a far stronger sense of forbiddance than the calmer entrance regions ever conveyed. Unlike the earlier impression of a quiet place wishing merely to remain undisturbed, these areas feel actively antagonistic in their configuration, giving a hostility toward anyone who leaves it alone in the darkness — unwelcoming. Others view it differently: it is sinister or the whole place that hides its sinisterness through continued intrusion. Beyond the darkness, there is believed to be nothing, aside from the ever-changing rooms and corridors extending without end. Further navigation is entirely discouraged as all known expeditions discovered nothing significant. Moreover, no other exits have ever been documented in this faraway land, so anyone, whether they have already done so or plan to reach these far-off locations, is strongly encouraged to return to the original entrance, as it is still the only known way out. Forgetting the way back could mean one becomes completely lost in this place. Importantly, once lost here, it is impossible to send messages or notify outsiders of one's whereabouts via any external communication — these include radio signals, network connections, or other means of contact. Your voice will be confined in here as long as you are still inside.









{| class="article-table" !FURTHER NOTICE #1: Individuals who enter this level are unable to locate any confirmed exit other than the entrance through which they originally arrived. All documented survivors encountered a direct physical entrance; no verified case of no-clipping into this level has ever been recorded. However, because communication with the outside is entirely impossible once inside, any individual who enters by unintended means would likely be unable to escape or report their condition. Numerous disappearances of unknown cause may be connected to this level, and while the possibility of accidental no-clipping remains unconfirmed, it cannot be dismissed. !
[[File:Question_mark.png|left|150px|link=]]
[[File:WhiteQuestion_mark.png|left|150px|link=]]
|}









{| class="article-table" !FURTHER NOTICE #2: If you find yourself no-clipping into here, continue moving forward. Do not remain stationary for too long; you never go anywhere, and however long it takes you to realize it, you always have to wander deep into the level, no matter what. Despite the presence of dead ends, the environment constantly extends indefinitely with new passages continually reemerging deeper within the darkness. No evidence confirms the existence of an exit farther ahead, although it is also possible that either there may be few or one in the faraway land or beyond. Lastly, if you unfortunately find yourself in this scenario, we wish you the best of luck in an escape, and may god acknowledge your hard work throughout the journey into the void — your effort will never go to waste, we believe. Keep walking. !
[[File:Question_mark.png|left|150px|link=]]
[[File:WhiteQuestion_mark.png|left|150px|link=]]
|}









{| class="article-table" !FURTHER NOTICE #3: May God be with you on your journey, and if you discover anything, good luck, in case there is still light or something out there deep in the abyss. !
[[File:Question_mark.png|left|150px|link=]]
[[File:WhiteQuestion_mark.png|left|150px|link=]]
|}
